summary: Backend-only smoke + contract tests against the deployed ScalpX FastAPI service. 27/27 tests pass. Verified: /api/ health; /api/presets list+single+404+PUT-persist (all 4 default keys present with required fields); /api/settings defaults and PUT-persist; /api/fx/inr-to-usd returns a positive numeric rate via open.er-api.com; /api/auth/login with garbage credentials returns 401 with detail starting 'Groww login failed:' (SDK call is wired); every protected /api/account/*, /api/instruments/{expiries,option-chain}, /api/auth/verify, /api/orders/{place-preset,exit} returns 401 'Missing X-Groww-Token header' without the header; with a fake X-Groww-Token, downstream Groww calls return clean JSON 401/502 (verified directly against the in-cluster URL http://localhost:8001 because the Cloudflare-fronted preview URL rewrites upstream 502s into HTML — backend itself is clean). /api/instruments/underlyings gracefully falls back to the static index list (NIFTY, BANKNIFTY, FINNIFTY, MIDCPNIFTY, SENSEX, BANKEX) when the Groww CSV cannot be loaded.

minor.issue: The instrument-master cache (_INSTRUMENTS_CACHE) is process-global and not keyed by token/identity. In a multi-user deployment, one user's CSV would leak to another. Acceptable for the documented single-user app, but worth a comment in code.

Backend correctly returns JSON 502 for upstream Groww failures, but the public Cloudflare-fronted host (quick-trade-73.preview.emergentagent.com) rewrites 502 responses into an HTML error page. Mobile clients should treat 502 as 'upstream failed, try again' rather than parse the body. Not fixable from FastAPI.
PRD.md says FX provider is exchangerate.host but code (correctly per latest request) uses open.er-api.com. PRD wording should be updated for accuracy.

action_items: Optional: add a docstring/comment near _INSTRUMENTS_CACHE noting it is single-user scope.
Optional: update PRD.md FX provider line from exchangerate.host to open.er-api.com to match implementation.

Real-credential paths (login success, account margin/positions/orders, expiries, option-chain, place-preset, exit) cannot be exercised here — user must validate end-to-end with their own Groww API key + TOTP secret from the Login screen.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 619 lines but cohesive; if it grows further consider splitting into routers (auth/account/instruments/orders/presets/settings).
_groww_client(token) constructs GrowwAPI per request — fine, but each call adds latency. Caching by token hash with a short TTL could speed up bursts.
All endpoints use _call_blocking(asyncio.to_thread) correctly to keep the event loop responsive while the sync growwapi SDK runs.
Pydantic models validate all bodies; no raw dict ingestion. MongoDB _id is consistently excluded from responses ({'_id': 0} projection). Good.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests use the seeded defaults from startup. Test mutations on presets and settings are restored at end of each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth-protected Groww paths cannot be exercised without a real Groww API key + base32 TOTP secret. For the deployed URL, Cloudflare rewrites 502 bodies into HTML — assert against http://localhost:8001 when you need to validate the JSON error envelope. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py is the regression suite.
rca_of_issue: n/a — no bugs to RCA. Initial test failures were caused by (a) Cloudflare ingress rewriting upstream 502s to HTML on the public URL, mitigated by hitting the in-cluster URL for those specific assertions; backend itself returns clean JSON in all paths.
